title: cocculus prainianus
intro: Cocculus prainianus, also known as the Borneo seed or snake fruit, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The plant has medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic effects, which contribute to its use in treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Cocculus prainianus include alleviating symptoms of fever, headache, and rheumatism, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and insomnia. Herbal preparations of Cocculus prainianus include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine and Ayurvedic practices.
